name: Chicken and Linguica Gumbo
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Linguica sausage, cut into 1/4-inch-thick rounds
Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
2 bunches finely chopped green onions (white and green parts)
1/2 cup finely chopped green bell pepper
Cayenne pepper and/or 2 to 6 teaspoons file powder, to taste
4 cups hot, freshly cooked rice for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook the sausage in a large, heavy ungreased skillet over low heat, turning the slices frequently with a spatula until there is a film of fat on the bottom of the pan.
2. Increase the heat to medium and continue frying the sausage, turning occasionally, until the slices are browned, about 6 minutes. Remove the sausage to paper towels to drain.
3. Add vegetable oil to the skillet to a depth of 1/2 inch.
4. Season the chicken pieces with salt and pepper.
5. Place half a cup of flour on a plate. Dredge each chicken piece in the flour, shaking off excess.
6. Heat the oil in the skillet over medium-high heat. Fry the chicken pieces until golden brown on both sides and cooked through, about 12 to 15 minutes per side.
7. Remove the chicken from the skillet and drain on paper towels.
8. Reserve 6 tablespoons of fat from the skillet and discard the rest.
9. Whisk the reserved fat with the remaining 6 tablespoons flour in a large, heavy pot until smooth.
10. Cook over medium heat, stirring frequently, until the fat-flour mixture (roux) is nut brown. This will take several minutes; do not allow the roux to burn.
11. Add green onions, celery, green bell pepper, parsley, and garlic to the roux. Stir the mixture until the vegetables have softened.
12. Slowly add chicken stock while stirring over medium-low heat. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat.
13. Add the sausage to the gumbo. Season with salt, pepper, cayenne, and/or Tabasco sauce as desired.
14. Simmer uncovered for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
15. While simmering, bone and shred the cooked chicken using fingers. Add it to the gumbo and continue simmering for an additional 15 minutes.
16. NOTE: The gumbo tastes best if prepared a day or two in advance and refrigerated. Let the gumbo cool completely at room temperature before refrigerating. Reheat over medium-low heat, stirring occasionally.
17. Just before serving, remove the gumbo from the heat, taste for seasoning (it should be spicy), and stir in file powder to taste. File powder turns stringy as it cooks; add it after removing the pot from the heat.
18. Transfer the gumbo to a heated tureen and serve with rice on a heated soup plate. Ladle the gumbo around the rice and garnish each serving with finely chopped green onions. Serves 8.
